message:
test(harness): conformance ext4 block & inode bitmap CRC32C vs e2fsck

ffs-ondisk has had verify_block_bitmap_checksum /
verify_inode_bitmap_checksum since the early metadata_csum work, but
the only conformance pin was an indirect comparison through the
group-descriptor's stored bitmap_csum field
(ext4_group_desc_kernel_reference, round 1). That test only proves
"ffs reads the same byte the kernel wrote" — it never recomputes the
CRC from the bitmap content and validates against the stored value.

New harness ext4_bitmap_csum_kernel_reference fills that gap:
  * Three image variants (4 KiB / 1 KiB blocks; with and without
    journal) so both 32-byte and 64-byte descriptor widths exercise
    the lo-only-half vs lo+hi half of bg_*_bitmap_csum.
  * For every group whose bitmap is initialised, reads the on-disk
    bitmap block, calls verify_*_bitmap_checksum (kernel-equivalent
    formula `crc32c(s_csum_seed, bitmap[..len])`), and asserts Ok.
  * Cross-checks block_bitmap_checksum_value /
    inode_bitmap_checksum_value (the value-only API) returns
    exactly the stored csum after width truncation.
  * Skips groups with BG_BLOCK_UNINIT or BG_INODE_UNINIT — the
    kernel does not stamp a csum into uninitialised bitmaps and
    leaves the field at 0; verifying those would compare a CRC over
    arbitrary disk content against literal zero.
  * Independently verifies via `e2fsck -fn`, so even a hypothetical
    case where ffs and the kernel both miscompute the same way is
    still caught by e2fsprogs disagreement.

Self-skips when ext4 userland is unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
